prompt:
With the rising popularity of people who dropped out of college and became successful, more and more young people tend to end their education process as soon as they graduate from school or even earlier. However, there are people who think that the trend to get higher education among younger generation is toxic and meaningless, because if you find a job early, you will be successful in life. 

Personally, I strongly disagree with that point of view. First, when a person graduates from school, he/she has extremely limited knowledge in almost every sphere and no experience in adult life. Getting an university degree can broaden your vision on a particular sphere of interest, help you gain knowledge and skills much more quickier than you'd have gained 'em while working in whatever company. Additionally, while a person studies at university, he/she learns how to live on their owns, separated from their parents, and the adaptation period goes much more easier than if a person decided to find a job right after graduating out of school. 

Also, university is a great place to skyrocket your social skills, soft-skills and common sense. That happens because you interact with people of your age with similar goals, hobbies, manner of talking, and that makes the process of the personality development smooth like butter. On the other hand, when you start working somewhere, there will be a little chance that you'll meet people your age or people you simply would like to interact with. In the end, we are still children at 18 y.o. 

In conclusion, I want to say that every single person should decide for themselves whether they need degree or not; whether they need to find a job after school or not. After all, every individual has their own perception on any decision in their life.

Use EXPERT_ANNOTATION, CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES, RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS, and RANKING_ANCHORS only as supporting context; never override direct evidence from the student text.
Return ONLY one CEFR label from: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2.
Do not output JSON.
Do not output explanation.

TASK: There is an opinion that too many young people now want to get a university degree. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.
STUDENT_TEXT: With the rising popularity of people who dropped out of college and became successful, more and more young people tend to end their education process as soon as they graduate from school or even earlier. However, there are people who think that the trend to get higher education among younger generation is toxic and meaningless, because if you find a job early, you will be successful in life. 

Personally, I strongly disagree with that point of view. First, when a person graduates from school, he/she has extremely limited knowledge in almost every sphere and no experience in adult life. Getting an university degree can broaden your vision on a particular sphere of interest, help you gain knowledge and skills much more quickier than you'd have gained 'em while working in whatever company. Additionally, while a person studies at university, he/she learns how to live on their owns, separated from their parents, and the adaptation period goes much more easier than if a person decided to find a job right after graduating out of school. 

Also, university is a great place to skyrocket your social skills, soft-skills and common sense. That happens because you interact with people of your age with similar goals, hobbies, manner of talking, and that makes the process of the personality development smooth like butter. On the other hand, when you start working somewhere, there will be a little chance that you'll meet people your age or people you simply would like to interact with. In the end, we are still children at 18 y.o. 

In conclusion, I want to say that every single person should decide for themselves whether they need degree or not; whether they need to find a job after school or not. After all, every individual has their own perception on any decision in their life. 
TEXT_TYPE: essay

What is your opinion?","student_text":"The author claims that success is explained only by a hard work. I am absolutely agree with this statement. In our big world each person has some talent. But each talent needs the development to reach success. I think that author means that, when he says that luck has nothing to do with a success. There are many people with the same talents and only the hard work can allow to win in this competition of talents. First and foremost, there are a cases when the person have some talent but not reach a success. My brother was a scientist and he had dreamed about studing in the prestigious foreign university. He was not silly çat all. His inteligence quality score is one hundred and fourty two. But he was lazy and he had not practiced with math. Thus, he had not passed math exam in any foreign university, but it was possible. Last but not the least, some people want to realise their dream so much that any negative circumstances cannot stop it. When my mother was a kid she wanted about be a doctor. Her dream was to be a surgeon, bur she had the disease with hand which made impossible her plans about profession. Surpisingly, now she is a surgeon. Step by step she have been treating her disease and of cource this was a hard work. State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.","student_text":"There is an opinion that young people should go to work after school. I cannnot agree with this point. Let me explain.

Of course, going to work early sounds good. A person begins to gain work experience, builds a career and earns money for himself. This is a good opportunity for people who are confident in themselves and have decided on the field in which they want to work.

But there is different situation. School graduates often don't know what they want to do and where they want to work. Or maybe they want to work in science or in other difficult area. In this situation university is the best choice. In the university people study different complex subjects. They can try various fields and choose what they like. And knowledge that university gives is complex.

To sum it up, there is no one right answer to this question.The choice depends on the situation and needs of a particular person.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":6,"errors":[{"text":"cannnot ","labels":["Spelling"],"start":72,"end":80},{"text":"different situation","labels":["Grammar"],"start":392,"end":411},{"text":" other","labels":["Grammar"],"start":540,"end":546},{"text":"In the university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":612,"end":629},{"text":"knowledge","labels":["Grammar"],"start":730,"end":739},{"text":" university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":744,"end":755},{"text":"To sum it up,","labels":["Discourse markers"],"start":775,"end":788},{"text":"there is no one right answer","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":789,"end":817},{"text":"needs","labels":["Grammar"],"start":875,"end":880}],"strengths":[{"text":"Of course,","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":120,"end":130},{"text":"But there is different situation.","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":379,"end":412},{"text":"Or maybe they want","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":497,"end":515},{"text":"They can try","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":671,"end":683}],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07013","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, im styduing in the MIPT - in one of the hardest technical universitiyes in Russia, and i want to give you some advices.

My first advidce - it to sleep from 7 to 9 hours (how much exactly depends from you) in day, and you should stand from up and go to bed in one time in day. With sleeping regulary in one time you will feel better and maybe you will feel better.

Next advice - it to make calendar with notions, or something like this, it helps to plan day wery succesfull. Sometimes procrastination wins you, but with notions it weel be easier to make hometasks in time.

The third advice - food delivery, if you have not enought time to cook something, or to go somewhere in big sity you can easily get food from delivery services. But sometimes people eats only food from delivery, and even don't go out from their rooms, of course it isn't good.

And the last what i want to say - probably your biggest troubles with time it that you have not hard character. Without this super ability it hard to self-controle, may be you should to ask your friends wathching what are you doing in free time, they can anounce if you spend time on games or other time-killers.
